intake.user_request: Scope bulletproof concept #4 of 6 (branded paths) for CODEREF-CORE. No stub predecessor — scoped directly in the same pattern as frozen-fixture (#2) and seam-deletion (#3) WOs. Problem: src/integration/rag/ uses raw string for all path values (basePath, filePath, fullPath, graphPath, reportPath). The runtime isAbsolute() guards at chunk-converter.ts:222-225 and indexing-orchestrator.ts:500 exist because the type system cannot distinguish absolute from relative paths. Fix: TypeScript branded types (AbsolutePath, RelativePath) that make absolute/relative confusion a compile-time error.
intake.predecessor_wo: WO-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001 (archived 2026-05-08, CORE cfd485a) — seam deletion including the silent fileExists skip (-28 LOC)

classification.plan_type_rationale: Phase 1 = path inventory (walk src/integration/rag/*.ts, enumerate every string parameter that is actually a path, classify each as absolute-only / relative-only / either; document which runtime isAbsolute() guards exist and why). Phase 1 is non-skippable: the exact brand classification per parameter is the load-bearing decision, and it cannot be enumerated at scoping time without risking misclassification. Phase 2 = define path-types.ts with AbsolutePath / RelativePath branded types + constructor functions. Phase 3 = propagate branded types through chunk-converter.ts and indexing-orchestrator.ts (replace raw string params, remove redundant isAbsolute runtime guards). Phase 4 = update __tests__ if any test constructs paths as raw strings that now need branding + closeout.
classification.plan_type_decided_by: SKILLS scoping (daily-agent-session-2026-05-08). SKILLS judges phased: the per-parameter classification (absolute/relative/either) is unknowable at scoping time without reading the source, and a misclassification would propagate a wrong brand silently through the type system — worse than the raw string status quo.
classification.discovery: Two confirmed runtime isAbsolute() guards at scoping time: (1) chunk-converter.ts:222-225 readFile() method: `const fullPath = path.isAbsolute(filePath) ? filePath : path.join(this.basePath, filePath)`. (2) indexing-orchestrator.ts:500 staleness check: `const abs = pathMod.isAbsolute(rel) ? rel : pathMod.join(this.basePath, rel)`. Both exist because callers pass relative node.file paths from graph.json but the type is string — no compile-time enforcement. No branded path types exist anywhere in src/ today (confirmed by grep at scoping). basePath field on ChunkConverter (line 24) and IndexingOrchestrator is raw string.

in_scope: Phase 1: walk src/integration/rag/*.ts and src/integration/rag/__tests__/*.ts; enumerate every string parameter that is actually a path; classify each as absolute-only / relative-only / either; document which runtime isAbsolute() guards exist and why; produce inventory.md in WO directory

Phase 2: define src/integration/rag/path-types.ts with AbsolutePath branded type, RelativePath branded type, toAbsolute() constructor, toRelative() constructor, and any additional helpers Phase 1 determines necessary (e.g., joinAbsolute(), isAbsolutePath() type guard)

Phase 3: propagate branded types through chunk-converter.ts — replace basePath: string with basePath: AbsolutePath on the class field and constructor; replace filePath: string in readFile() with RelativePath; replace groupNodesByFile node.file parameter types where applicable; remove redundant path.isAbsolute() runtime guards that become type-enforced

Phase 3 (continued): propagate branded types through indexing-orchestrator.ts — replace basePath: string with basePath: AbsolutePath where it appears; brand the rel variable from graph.json node.file as RelativePath; remove redundant pathMod.isAbsolute() runtime guard at line 500; update normalizeChunkFileForGraphJoin() signature if basePath param is included

Phase 4: update src/integration/rag/__tests__/*.ts if any test passes raw string literals where branded types are now required; update path-bearing test fixtures if applicable (excluding frozen-fixture dir which is frozen); closeout-ready communication.json update
path-types.ts exports are ONLY consumed within src/integration/rag/ — if any caller outside the boundary needs branding, HALT and report
out_of_scope: Phase 7 archive — locked, MUST NOT be reopened

Concept #5 (schema reduction) and concept #6 (two-grain drop) — separate WOs

confirmed_runtime_guards_at_scoping.code: const fullPath = path.isAbsolute(filePath) ? filePath : path.join(this.basePath, filePath);
confirmed_runtime_guards_at_scoping.why_guard_exists: filePath comes from graph.json node.file (project-relative string), but the parameter type is raw string — callers could accidentally pass an absolute path or an already-joined path. Guard defends against double-join.
confirmed_runtime_guards_at_scoping.brand_fix: filePath: RelativePath parameter on readFile(); this.basePath: AbsolutePath on constructor; path.join(this.basePath, filePath) always produces AbsolutePath — isAbsolute guard becomes redundant and can be removed.

confirmed_runtime_guards_at_scoping.code: const abs = pathMod.isAbsolute(rel) ? rel : pathMod.join(this.basePath, rel);
confirmed_runtime_guards_at_scoping.why_guard_exists: rel is graph.json node.file — should always be relative per graph.json contract, but the raw string type cannot enforce it. Guard defends against the same double-join bug.
confirmed_runtime_guards_at_scoping.brand_fix: rel: RelativePath local variable (branded from node.file cast); this.basePath: AbsolutePath; pathMod.join() produces AbsolutePath — isAbsolute guard becomes redundant and can be removed.

evidence_summary.confirmed_no_branded_types_in_src: grep -r '__brand' src/ returns zero hits at scoping time. No AbsolutePath, RelativePath, or branded path types exist anywhere in src/.
evidence_summary.basepath_field_current_type: ChunkConverter.basePath: string (chunk-converter.ts:24). IndexingOrchestrator basePath: string (confirmed by constructor signature at indexing-orchestrator.ts; exact line TBD in Phase 1 inventory).
evidence_summary.graph_node_file_is_relative: graph.json node.file keys are project-relative paths (confirmed by normalizeChunkFileForGraphJoin docstring at indexing-orchestrator.ts:38-42 and chunk-converter.ts groupNodesByFile usage). These are the RelativePath inputs.
evidence_summary.call_sites_that_set_basepath: Phase 1 inventory must enumerate every external call site that constructs ChunkConverter(basePath) or IndexingOrchestrator({basePath}) within src/integration/rag/ to determine if callers pass absolute paths (which they should) — these call sites are where toAbsolute() must be applied.
